title: lithospermum turneri
intro: Lithospermum turneri, also known as Turner's gromwell, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains compounds like lithospermic acid and rosmarinic acid, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Lithospermum turneri can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
